name: Optex Systems Holdings Inc
business: We manufacture optical sighting systems and assemblies for the U.S. Department of Defense ( DoD ), foreign military applications, commercial markets, and consumer markets. Our products are installed on a variety of U.S. military land vehicles, such as the Abrams and Bradley fighting vehicles, light armored and advanced security vehicles and the Stryker family of vehicles. We also manufacture and deliver numerous periscope configurations, rifle and surveillance sights and night vision optical assemblies.

exposures.sentence: We manufacture optical sighting systems and assemblies for the U.S. Department of Defense ( DoD ), foreign military applications, commercial markets, and consumer markets.

Our products are installed on a variety of U.S. military land vehicles, such as the Abrams and Bradley fighting vehicles, light armored and advanced security vehicles and the Stryker family of vehicles.
On June 29, 2023, the Effective Date , we executed a License agreement LA12342 with Pratt & Whitney Division ( P&W ) of Raytheon Technologies Corporation for a six-year term from the Effective date.

The Optex Outdoors webstore offers a curated selection of advanced optical products, including riflescope prisms, chronographs, and stabilized viewing products designed specifically for the needs of elite shooters and outdoor enthusiasts.
Globally, commercial optical products use thin film coatings to create product differentiation.

catalysts.event: Appointment of Chad George as Chief Executive Officer
catalysts.sentence: on December 5, 2025, the Board of Directors of the Company (the Board ) appointed Chad George, the Company s President, to assume the additional role of Chief Executive Officer to fill the vacancy left by Mr. Schoening, effective as of the Effective Date.

catalysts.event: New contract award for optical sub-assemblies
catalysts.sentence: On December 15, 2025, the Company announced it was awarded a new contract for optical sub-assemblies.

catalysts.event: Purchase order from General Dynamics Land Systems - Canada for laser protected periscopes
catalysts.sentence: On August 5, 2025, the Company announced it was awarded a firm fixed price purchase order from General Dynamics Land Systems - Canada.

catalysts.event: Five-year requirement-type contract for Abrams-based optical sighting systems
catalysts.sentence: On July 22, 2025 the Company announced it was awarded an up to $10.2 million, five-year requirement-type contract by the Army Contracting Command - Detroit Arsenal for Abrams-based optical sighting systems.

catalysts.event: Purchase order for XM30 Combat Vehicle sighting systems
catalysts.sentence: On July 7, 2025 the Company announced it was awarded a $2.8 million purchase order from a major U.S. prime contractor in support of the XM30 Combat Vehicle.

catalysts.event: Contract for laser filter units for XM157 Next Generation Fire Control Scope
catalysts.sentence: On April 9, 2025 the Company announced it was awarded a new contract for laser filter units associated with the XM157 Next Generation Fire Control Scope.

catalysts.event: Purchase order from BAE Systems for Bradley A4 periscopes and optical sighting systems
catalysts.sentence: On March 19, 2025 the Company announced it was awarded a $4.2 million firm fixed price purchase order from BAE Systems in support of the Bradley A4 Follow-On Production award for laser protected periscopes and optical sighting systems.

catalysts.event: Renewal of credit facility with Texas Capital Bank
catalysts.sentence: On May 21, 2025, the Company and Optex Systems, Inc. renewed their existing credit facility with the Lender by entering into a new Business Loan Agreement (the Loan Agreement ) effective May 22, 2025.

catalysts.event: Expiration of IDIQ contract ordering period (SPE7LX21D0057)
catalysts.sentence: On October 7, 2024, second option was exercised by DLA Land and Maritime, extending the ordering period from January 6, 2025 through January 5, 2026.

tamClaims.claim: GPO projects total DoD spending at $961.7 billion for fiscal year 2026, an increase of 11.6% over 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: for fiscal year 2026, the Government Publishing Office ( GPO ) projects total DoD spending at $961.7 billion, an overall increase of 11.6% over estimated fiscal year 2025 spending.

tamClaims.claim: Defense procurement spending projected to increase by $30.8 billion or 17.7% in fiscal year 2026.
tamClaims.sentence: For defense procurement spending, a subset of total defense spending, the GPO projects an overall increase of $30.8 billion, or 17.7%, in fiscal year 2026 as compared to the estimated spending in fiscal year 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Total fiscal year 2026 budget request for major ground system programs is $4,393.9 million.
